findings.problem: Sanitization drift: Clay's politically convenient detention of Deshawn is rendered as report language (radio fragment and hallway recap) instead of embodied scene consequence, which defuses institutional violence at the exact pressure point where the chapter's moral stakes peak.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:269, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:271, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:273, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:399, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:403, replace summary-only reporting with on-page concrete consequence: who physically takes Deshawn, what legal pretext is spoken, who objects, and what immediate harm/risk follows in the room.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if revised material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:269-273 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:399-403 shows at least one observable detention action (physical handling or restraint) and one explicit rights/process breach witnessed by a character; FAIL if Deshawn's detention remains only reported via static or quoted recap.

findings.problem: The chapter lands a major reversal (Thomas dead, Isaiah staged again) but with too few immediate causal breadcrumbs in-scene, so the reveal reads more as imposed twist than investigative inevitability.
findings.rewrite_direction: In the service-corridor discovery block anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:157, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:165, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:177, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:195, add 2-3 specific, non-solving clues (entry vector, struggle geometry, timing indicator, tool trace, camera/power tamper sign) that constrain how the killing and staging could have happened.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if a careful reader can cite at least one plausible attacker-access path and one timing anchor from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:157-195 without external chapters; FAIL if the scene still provides only body presence plus command reaction.

findings.problem: Clay's dialogue over-reuses the same abstract institutional lexicon (continuity/stability/order/protect) with similar cadence, creating style-bible token-list echo rather than evolving rhetorical pressure and leverage.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Clay's turns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:73, 205, 217, 227, 231, and 371 so each move has distinct register intent (camera polish, operational directive, private coercion) and varied syntax rather than repeated abstract-noun framing.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if each Clay line in the cited set performs a different leverage function and no abstract governance noun pair is repeated across more than two of those turns; FAIL if his speech still reads as interchangeable institutional boilerplate.

findings.problem: Recurring 'X looked ...' sentence scaffolding appears well beyond twice and becomes a prose tic, flattening rhythm and reducing sensory variety.
findings.rewrite_direction: At the cited lines in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md, replace at least five 'looked' constructions with differentiated perception/action beats (sound-first, touch-first, inference-first, blocking-first) while preserving scene logic.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if the revised cited spans contain no more than two finite 'looked' constructions and each replacement still clearly orients speaker position and attention target; FAIL if the repeated observation template remains the dominant transition device.

findings.problem: Interiority drought: the run from Mara's corridor challenge through the paramedic beat plays as external camera feed for 10+ consecutive paragraphs, weakening close-third anchoring to Lena.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:221, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:227, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:243, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:245, insert at least two brief Lena-internal beats (thought, bodily sensation, memory flash, or self-assessment) at turning points: Clay's quote, Mara's preservation line, and/or the body drape moment.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS only if snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:221-245 includes at least two explicit Lena-internal lines and no run longer than nine consecutive paragraphs without interiority; FAIL if the segment remains purely external reportage.

summary: Chapter 14 delivers strong sensory panic and lands required state deltas, but it fails review on unresolved medium+ craft risks: a sanitized off-page detention beat, under-seeded immediate causality at the murder reveal, mechanical Clay rhetoric, repeated prose templates, and a sustained interiority dropout.
